page: Amazon Web Services p6-b200.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by 192 dedicated Intel vCPUs, equivalent to 96 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this system features 2048.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs with a combined GPU VRAM of 1432 GB. Local storage is provided via 30400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware configuration represents a high-density resource allocation, combining massive GPU memory and local NVMe storage. This profile trades off lower-cost general-purpose compute for specialized, high-throughput parallel processing capabilities. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention.
This server is structured for large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing simulations, and data-intensive parallel processing workloads.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This hardware profile prioritizes massive GPU memory and local storage capacity over general-purpose utility, making it a specialized platform for resource-intensive workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution without resource sharing. It is structured for large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning inference, and high-performance computing simulations.
